mechanism_narrative: A sustained rise in the effective federal funds rate over the trailing three months raises the discount rate applied to expected equity cash flows and tightens broad financial conditions, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days regardless of the prevailing macro regime.
evidence_refs: fact:FEDFUNDS at 1.91% in Aug-2018, up from prior year, consistent with ongoing tightening cycle
failure_condition: Rank IC between trailing 3-month FEDFUNDS change and 20d forward SPX returns stays below 0.03 over any 3-year rolling window, or forward returns following rate increases are not significantly negative (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: When the unemployment rate falls below 4.0% and remains there for at least two consecutive months, the labor market is tight enough to generate wage-driven inflation pressure that forces the Federal Reserve into more aggressive tightening, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the following quarter.
candidate_trigger: UNRATE falls below 4.0% and stays below for 2 consecutive months
evidence_refs: fact:UNRATE recorded at 3.8-3.9% in Aug-2018, near multi-decade lows
regime_hint: late-cycle expansion with tight labor market
failure_condition: SPX 60-day forward returns are positive in more than 55% of triggered sub-4.0% UNRATE episodes historically, or Rank IC < 0.03 in any 3-year rolling window when the trigger is active.

mechanism_narrative: When housing starts (HOUST) decline on a 3-month basis while retail sales (RSAFS) continue to rise, the divergence signals that consumption is being sustained by non-housing, potentially debt-financed spending rather than broad-based demand, an unsustainable configuration that precedes negative S&P 500 forward returns over 20 trading days.
evidence_refs: fact:HOUST revised value 1208K vs vintage 1168K showing softening housing starts trend in mid-2018 while RSAFS vintage value 509,025M continued to climb
failure_condition: The HOUST-declining/RSAFS-rising divergence pattern precedes negative 20d SPX returns less than 50% of the time across the 2005-2024 sample, or Rank IC < 0.03 over any 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: During a monetary tightening cycle (rising FEDFUNDS), a deceleration in year-over-year industrial production (INDPRO) growth signals that higher financing costs are beginning to compress corporate output and margins, leading to negative S&P 500 forward returns over the following quarter. Outside of tightening regimes, the same INDPRO deceleration has muted or reversed predictive power.
evidence_refs: fact:INDPRO vintage value 108.23 vs revised 104.03 in Aug-2018 amid ongoing FEDFUNDS increases to 1.91%
failure_condition: INDPRO YoY deceleration during FEDFUNDS-rising periods is not followed by below-median 60d SPX returns in at least 55% of historical tightening-cycle instances, or Rank IC < 0.03 restricted to tightening regimes.

mechanism_narrative: When the effective federal funds rate crosses above the 2.00% threshold, markets interpret this as confirmation of a genuine policy-tightening regime rather than gradual normalization, producing a short-lived risk-off reaction and negative S&P 500 forward returns over the following 5 trading days.
evidence_refs: fact:FEDFUNDS at 1.91% in Aug-2018, approaching the 2.00% threshold ahead of the anticipated Sep-2018 FOMC hike
failure_condition: 5-day forward SPX returns following a FEDFUNDS crossing above 2.00% are not significantly negative (t-test p > 0.05) across historical crossing events, or fewer than 50% of such crossings show negative returns.

mechanism_narrative: Sustained positive month-over-month growth in total nonfarm payrolls (PAYEMS) reflects continued labor income generation supporting consumer spending and corporate revenue expectations, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days as an always-on trend-following signal.
evidence_refs: fact:PAYEMS vintage value 149,279K in Aug-2018 continuing multi-year uptrend
failure_condition: Rank IC between trailing 3-month PAYEMS growth and 20d forward SPX returns falls below 0.03 over any 3-year rolling window, or positive PAYEMS growth months are not associated with above-median forward returns.

mechanism_narrative: When retail sales (RSAFS) growth outpaces real GDP (GDPC1) growth over a two-quarter window, consumption is being sustained disproportionately by credit expansion or drawdown of savings rather than income growth, an imbalance that precedes negative S&P 500 forward returns over the following quarter as the imbalance corrects.
evidence_refs: fact:RSAFS vintage value 509,025M in Aug-2018 continuing to expand alongside a large GDPC1 revision from 18,514.6B to 20,150.5B highlighting demand-growth divergence risk
failure_condition: RSAFS growth exceeding GDPC1 growth over two quarters is not followed by below-median 60d SPX returns in at least 55% of historical instances, or Rank IC < 0.03 restricted to divergence periods.

mechanism_narrative: During a monetary tightening cycle, accelerating year-over-year CPI inflation alongside a rising federal funds rate signals that the central bank risks policy overshoot, generating near-term equity volatility and negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days. Outside tightening regimes, CPI acceleration alone does not carry the same signal.
evidence_refs: fact:CPIAUCSL vintage value 251.846 in Aug-2018 rising alongside FEDFUNDS at 1.91% ahead of expected further hikes
regime_hint: monetary tightening cycle with accelerating inflation
failure_condition: Joint CPIAUCSL acceleration and FEDFUNDS increases are not followed by significantly negative 5d SPX returns (t-test p > 0.05) across historical tightening-cycle instances, or Rank IC < 0.03 restricted to such joint-condition periods.
